structure.format: Multiple-choice questions including standalone and stimulus-based linked sets. Calculator allowed. Reference info (formulas, equations) available.

2 Long FRQs (8-10 points each, 25 min suggested) + 4 Short FRQs (4 points each, 10 min suggested). Paragraph-form answers required (outlines/bullets/diagrams alone NOT acceptable). Diagrams may supplement only.

frq_structure.long_frq_1: Interpreting and Evaluating Experimental Results (data analysis, modify experiment, justify hypothesis)
frq_structure.long_frq_2: Interpreting and Evaluating Experimental Results with Graphing (construct/interpret graph, predict effect)
frq_structure.short_frq_3: Scientific Investigation (identify control, state null hypothesis, justify claim)
frq_structure.short_frq_4: Conceptual Analysis (describe genetic evidence, explain evolutionary mechanism)
frq_structure.short_frq_5: Analyze a Model or Visual Representation (interpret diagram, explain regulation)
frq_structure.short_frq_6: Analyze Data (identify pattern, support hypothesis with data)

exam.reference_info_provided: equations and formulas accessible throughout the exam

science_practices: 1. Concept Explanation (describe biological concepts and processes)
2. Visual Representations (analyze visual diagrams, models, representations)
3. Questions and Methods (analyze scientific questions and methods, identify variables, identify control)
4. Representing and Describing Data (construct/interpret graphs, tables, data displays)
5. Statistical Tests and Data Analysis (chi-square, error bars, mean ± SE)
6. Argumentation (justify claim with evidence, support hypothesis)
course_themes: Big Idea 3 — Information Storage and Transmission
vocabulary_cues: Identify the dependent/independent variable in the experiments
Justify why the researchers included the control
Based on Figure X, describe the effect on Y when
Using data from Figure X, support the researchers' claim
Justify the researchers' claim based on your understanding of
Construct an appropriate type of graph that represents the data
Predict the effect of this mutation on
Use evidence from the information provided to support the scientists' claim
Explain how X could affect Y in such a way that Z
frq_command_terms: Explain (provide reasoning for cause-effect or mechanism)
Predict (state expected outcome based on principle)
Support (use evidence to back a claim)
Construct (build a graph, diagram, or representation)
sample_stems_frq: FRQ1 Long: Protein transport to ER via SR vs Sec62. Includes Figure 1 (bar graph of relative protein content with error bars), Figure 2 (3 proteins transport %). Parts A-D, subparts i/ii/iii covering: ribosome function, dependent variable ID, control justification, effect description, IV ID, calculation (495-234=261 nucleotides = 87 amino acid difference), support claim with data, justify amino-terminus signal.

FRQ2 Long: Moth pheromone signaling. G protein-coupled receptor DopEcR + 20E. Table 1 + Figure 1 signaling pathway. Parts A-D: polarity of membrane portion, construct graph from Table 1, identify activity affected, treatment group with >50% oriented, predict mutation effect, support claim with evidence, explain inhibitor as pesticide.
FRQ3 Short: Buffelgrass invasive species + saguaro cactus. Keystone species removal, control group ID, null hypothesis, justify wildfire claim.
FRQ4 Short: Isthmus of Panama formation + evolution. Genetic evidence of evolution, divergent evolution explain, predict resource availability effect, justify prediction.
FRQ5 Short: Enzymatic pathway A→intermediates→B with feedback loop. Active site characteristic, feedback inhibition explain, identify product, pH effect on enzyme 3.

FRQ6 Short: ald gene + meiosis. Western blot + bar graph of metaphase cells. Identify genotype at ~12%, describe protein production difference, support haploinsufficiency hypothesis, explain phenotype differences despite same protein.
